journal_entry.rationale: Utility services have been consumed but not yet paid. Under accrual accounting (IFRS), the expense must be recognized in the period incurred. Debit Utilities Expense to record the cost of services used (expense increase, slot c=1). Credit Accounts Payable to recognize the obligation to pay the utility provider (liability increase, slot a=1). Utilities are generally exempt from HST/GST as they are regulated services, so no tax line is added. Total debits ($50) equal total credits ($50).

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recording an expense and a liability. The credit side records the liability (accounts payable or accrued expenses) which is a liability increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recording an expense and a liability. The credit side records the liability (accounts payable or accrued expenses) which is a liability increase.
